notes: àm-àm = somewhat dark / pretty late.

english: not inferior to / no less than / like
notes: khah su = inferior to (in some quality).

english: to content oneself with / do in a half-hearted, aimless way
notes: bóng-kóng = just try to say it / talk at random; bóng-khì = might as well go.

english: to slice / cut into pieces; (of a switch) turn on or off by rotating
notes: Distinct from chūn. chhiat-sit / chhiat-hoa = turn off.
chhiùⁿ-koa = sing a song.

english: program / items on a program; plans (for the day / a date)
to turn / turn on (by rotating around a center) / to wring / twist
notes: Distinguish from chhiat. Used for radios, watches, faucets, wringing laundry.

english: to go out / die out (as light or fire)
notes: Often used as enclitic with verb. Used for appliances such as radio / TV.

english: to worry / be anxious / be uneasy
to feel at ease / have peace of mind / be free from anxiety

notes: Noun + í-gōa = besides / except for (that noun).
Often of time or quantity.

english: to win / gain a victory / to defeat / excel
notes: khah ... iâⁿ = to be superior to. Opposite: su.

english: Verb + ē / bē + iâⁿ — can / cannot (carry, hold, manage) + Verb
notes: Capacity / endurance construction. See Gram. Note 1B (p.369).
Measure word: ki / pha.

english: Verb + ē / bē + liáu — able / unable to (finish / handle) + Verb
notes: See Gram. Note 1A.

english: add / increase / additional / too much / too many / more / plus
notes: ke chi̍t ê = one more.

english: more or less / as much as possible / at least

notes: Lit. "open-shut". súi-chih is loanword from Japanese "suicchi".

english: to lessen / diminish / decrease / subtract / be missing
notes: Opposite of ke.
Measure word: lia̍p. phah-kiû = play ball; bāng-kiû = tennis; nâ-kiû = basketball; pâi-kiû = volleyball; pâng-kiû = ping-pong; toh-kiû / phin-phóng = table tennis.
kng-kng = rather bright (of light).
Measure word: tiâu. liû-hêng-koa = popular song; kok-koa = national anthem.
Distinct from completion marker liáu. liáu-chîⁿ = lose money; liáu sî-kan = waste time.
bô lō͘-ēng / bô lō·-iōng = useless.
Comparative intensifier. Often used twice (see #30).
lú lâi lú ... = as time goes on / more and more.
Often means 'replace' (one thing with another) rather than alter.
Variants: pîⁿ / pê· / pî·.

english: contest / game / tournament; to take part in a contest

notes: Distinct from sêng-mēng / éng-oa̍h = eternal / mortal life (Christian usage).

english: the electricity goes off / power failure
notes: Unintended power loss. Contrast thêng-tiān (intentional shutoff).
Measure word: tiâu. tiān-sòaⁿ = electric wire; pó-hiám-sòaⁿ = fuse wire.

english: (particle) — often indicates contrast / statement of unexpected happening
notes: Often expresses surprise or ridicule. Distinguish from soah = "finish" in Ls. 19.

english: to lose (a contest or any competition)
notes: Verb + su = lose (in the competition indicated by the verb). Opposite: iâⁿ.

english: have a safe and pleasant trip
notes: Said to someone leaving the speaker's home. Reply: ū sî-kan chiah koh lâi ("come again when you have time").
Generally synonymous with it-tēng.

english: to cut off the power supply
notes: Intentional shutoff by the power company. Contrast sit-tiān.
tiám tiān-hóe = turn on the light.
sio-phah-tiān = short circuit; hō· tiān tiān-tio̍h = to get an electric shock.
Measure word: pha. tiān-hóe khai-koan = light switch.
Measure word: lia̍p. Variants: tiān-kiû-á. chek = watts.

english: to break / sever (as a stick or thread)
notes: Usually for something with measure word ki or tiâu.

english: to blaze / be burning / (of light) go on / be on
notes: tiān-hóe toh = the light is on.

english: there is (are) place(s) where (a given action can be performed)
notes: See Gram. Note 2 (p.369). Counterpart: bô-tàng.

english: athletics / sports / to take physical exercise
